name: @radzor/csv-import
description: Parse, validate, and batch-import CSV files. Supports streaming for large files, schema validation with typed coercion, error row collection, and progress tracking. Works with file uploads, S3 streams, and local paths.

inputs.name: delimiter
inputs.description: Field delimiter character. Use '\t' for TSV.

inputs.name: hasHeader
inputs.description: Whether the first row contains column headers.

inputs.name: batchSize
inputs.description: Number of rows to emit per onBatch event and database insert.

inputs.name: skipEmptyRows
inputs.description: Skip rows where all fields are empty.

inputs.name: schema
inputs.description: Column type map for coercion and validation. Keys are column names.

outputs.name: parseStats
outputs.description: Total rows, valid rows, error rows, and processing duration.

events.name: onBatch
events.description: Fired when a batch of rows is ready for processing.

events.name: onComplete
events.description: Fired when the entire file has been parsed.

events.name: onError
events.description: Fired for each row that fails validation or parsing.

actions.name: parseBuffer
actions.description: Parse an in-memory CSV buffer and return all rows.

actions.name: parseStream
actions.description: Stream-parse a CSV file, emitting onRow and onBatch events progressively.

actions.name: validate

params.name: rows
params.description: Pre-parsed rows to validate against the schema.

actions.description: Validate rows against the configured schema, returning valid rows and error details.

actions.name: getHeaders
actions.description: Peek at the first row and return column header names.

llm.constraints: For files over 50MB, always use parseStream() instead of parseBuffer() to avoid memory issues. The schema coercion casts strings to types but does not validate ranges or formats — add your own business logic after parsing. date fields are coerced with new Date() — ensure dates are in ISO 8601 or MM/DD/YYYY format.
